title: The Antique Shop Clerk
learningGoal: The learner will verbally formulate sentences describing spatial locations, negative adjectives, and animate/inanimate existence in a conversational setting.
instructions: In this roleplay, you are a clerk at an antique shop. A customer asks you about items in the store. Reply verbally in Japanese based on the English prompts. Pay attention to verb choices (Arimasu vs Imasu) and adjective endings.

contentItems.text: Speak clearly. When you say an adjective is NOT something (e.g. not expensive), focus on the 'ku arimasen' ending.

speakingPrompt: Translate: 1) The book is on top of the desk. 2) It is not expensive. 3) The cat is under the tree.
scenario: A customer is looking around your shop and asking questions.

subTasks.prompt: The customer asks where the book is. Say: 'The book is on top of the desk.'

sampleResponse.targetText: ほんは つくえの うえに あります。
sampleResponse.transliteration: Hon wa tsukue no ue ni arimasu.
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: The book is on top of the desk.

subTasks.prompt: The customer asks if the book is expensive. Say: 'No, it is not expensive.'

sampleResponse.targetText: いいえ、たかくありません。

subTasks.prompt: The customer sees an animal outside. Clarify by saying: 'The cat is under the tree.'

sampleResponse.targetText: ねこは きの したに います。
sampleResponse.transliteration: Neko wa ki no shita ni imasu.
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: The cat is under the tree.

scenarioContext.targetText: あの ほんは どこですか。
scenarioContext.transliteration: Ano hon wa doko desu ka.

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: ほんは つくえの うえに あります。
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Hon wa tsukue no ue ni arimasu.
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: The book is on top of the desk.

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: いいえ、たかくありません。
ねこは きの したに います。
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Neko wa ki no shita ni imasu.
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: The cat is under the tree.

expectedSpeakingPoints: Say Hon wa tsukue no ue ni arimasu
Say Neko wa ki no shita ni imasu
acceptableParaphrases: Using 'ga' instead of 'wa' is perfectly acceptable (e.g. Neko ga...).
Dropping 'Iie' in the second sentence is fine as long as 'takaku arimasen' is used.

criteria.name: Existence Verb Accuracy
criteria.description: Did the learner use 'arimasu' for the book and 'imasu' for the cat?

criteria.name: Negative i-Adjective
criteria.description: Did the learner correctly conjugate 'takai' to 'takaku arimasen'?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: ほんは つくえの うえに あります。いいえ、たかくありません。ねこは きの したに います。

feedbackRules.onPass: Excellent roleplay! You flawlessly handled the existence verbs and adjective conjugations.
feedbackRules.onFail: Make sure you used 'Imasu' for the cat. Cats are alive, so they cannot use 'Arimasu'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! Remember that to make 'Takai' negative, you must drop the 'i' and say 'Takaku arimasen'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: あの ほんは どこですか。ほんは つくえの うえに あります。いいえ、たかくありません。ねこは きの したに います。

hintText: Focus on the endings: u-e-ni a-ri-ma-su. shi-ta-ni i-ma-su.
